name: POPPY MATCHA
tagline: Ceremonial-grade matcha kiosk with Memphis soul. Pop in, take out, stand out.
style_mood: memphis design, pastel mint + hot pink + mustard yellow, bold geometric patterns, 80s playful
target_audience: Gen Z and millennial office workers, wellness-oriented consumers, Instagram food lovers in urban Amsterdam food courts and shopping hubs
usps: Plug-and-play modular kiosk — 3 days to install, zero renovation commitment
Ceremonial-grade matcha sourced directly from Uji, Japan (€0.80/serving COGS)
Instagrammable Memphis design = organic social reach
€0 wasted on walls or floors you can't take with you when lease ends
Break-even at 42 cups/day (food court sees 2000+ daily footfall)

budget.renovation_note: Renovation is zero by design. Food court kiosks are leased on rolling contracts — any money spent on walls, flooring, or fixed installations stays with the landlord when you leave. All €12k goes into equipment, modular kiosk unit, and inventory — assets you physically take to the next location if this one doesn't work out. This is the #1 rule of low-budget launches: never invest in what you can't unscrew and carry out.

equipment.name: Modular kiosk unit (4-sided service counter)
equipment.model: MKU-08 Compact, 2.5×3m, pastel pink powder coat
equipment.why: Pre-fabricated, wheeled, assembles in 4 hours. Modular = takes apart and moves when lease ends. This IS the shop.

equipment.name: Matcha whisking station (electric chasen)
equipment.why: Hand-whisk ritual is visual theater but slow at peak. Electric hits 60 cups/hour.

equipment.name: Milk steamer & foamer
equipment.why: No espresso machine needed for matcha lattes — Bellman does milk foam for €240 vs €2000 for a Linea Mini.

equipment.name: Under-counter refrigerator
equipment.why: For milk alternatives (oat, almond, soy) + prepared ice matcha. 130L is enough for 2 service days.

equipment.name: Water filter system
equipment.why: Matcha is 99% water — filter is non-negotiable for ceremonial grade. BWT removes chlorine + softens.

equipment.name: Hot water boiler (2L)
equipment.why: Matcha needs precise 75–80°C. Cheap kettles overboil and scorch the tea.

equipment.name: Digital scale (0.1g precision)
equipment.why: Ceremonial dosing is 2.0g ±0.1g — Acaia is the barista standard.

equipment.name: POS tablet + card reader
equipment.why: SumUp Solo Lite: €0 monthly, 1.69% per transaction. iPad syncs with inventory and daily sales.

equipment.name: Signage + LED menu board
equipment.model: Acrylic Memphis panels + 32" LED digital menu
equipment.why: Menu board shows live sold-out state. Memphis acrylic is the visual hook — this is what people photograph.

tasks: Register eenmanszaak (sole trader) at KvK online — €80.10 fee, same-day
Request BTW number from Belastingdienst (automatic after KvK, 5 days)
Open zakelijk bank account (bunq or Knab, same-day digital)
Apply for Warenwethygiëne (food hygiene) certificate — €60 online course
Sign food court kiosk lease (Amsterdam Foodhallen, De Hallen, or Bajes Kwartier): €800/mo + 10% revenue share common
Confirm electrical amperage (min 16A) and water hookup with landlord
Submit HACCP self-control plan to GGD Amsterdam (free, 1 week review)
Order modular kiosk from IKON Display (3-week lead time — order early)
Order matcha inventory: 2kg ceremonial grade from Marukyu Koyamaen (€580 shipping incl.)
Order all equipment from single supplier (HorecaTraders NL) for unified warranty
Commission signage/menu board from local sign shop
Receive equipment, test all units in owner's home/storage
Photograph product shots for social/menu
Design packaging (cups, bamboo stirrers, stickers) — Pakhulp.nl print run €280
Kiosk delivered and assembled on-site (4 hours)
Install equipment, water filter, POS
Dry run: serve 20 test customers (friends/family) for free
Train backup barista (1 day, €150)
Soft launch: 50% off first 100 cups, Instagram ad €200
Local food bloggers outreach (5 Amsterdam accounts)
Google My Business listing + Foursquare + Apple Maps
Loyalty card: 10th cup free
Grand opening Friday 8am — full menu, full price
First-week metrics review: cups/hour, peak times, break-even math
Adjust inventory order cadence based on real consumption

documents_required: Valid passport or EU ID card
Business activity description (matching SBI code 56.10.2 — fast food)

tax.small_business_scheme: KOR — if turnover <€20k, VAT-exempt (optional, reduces admin)

social_security.note: No mandatory social security contributions for ZZP — buy private health insurance (~€135/mo) separately

activity_permits: HACCP food safety plan (self-drafted, GGD review free)
Food court sublease agreement with landlord
Package waste producer registration (Afvalfonds Verpakkingen, if turnover >€100k/yr — likely skip year 1)

pnl.notes: Assumes 70 cups/day × €5 average × 24 working days = €8,400/mo. Owner works 5 days/week, part-time helper €9/hr × 20 hrs/wk.
